title: Dischidia incrassata
taxonomy: Dischidia incrassata is classified as a type of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. This family of plants is commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. Dischidia incrassata is part of the genus Dischidia, which consists of tropical climbers with ovate leaves. These plants are native to Australia and Asia and are often used in indoor gardening due to their ability to thrive in low-light conditions. They are also known for their ability to tolerate drought and heat.
